utility_evaluation.REASONING: The required format lists 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, and total_budget. In the final coverage plan, all 5 items are present. Each has the required descriptive details: (1) health_coverage includes physical and mental health insurance and specific costs (monthly_cost and coverage details), (2) property_coverage includes home/contents insurance but lacks explicit cost, which is a missing detail, (3) travel_coverage includes travel insurance but also lacks explicit cost, (4) additional_services includes enhancement services but without cost, (5) total_budget includes monthly and annual cost breakdown, so it is fully specified. Thus, only health_coverage and total_budget are fully detailed with costs. The other three are missing cost information.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The AI assistant’s final coverage plan lists 'LifeFirst Standard Plan5' as the health coverage. The ground-truth ratings include 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' (rating 8) for both physical and mental health, which clearly corresponds despite the trailing '5' in the name. For property coverage, the plan provides 'Luxury home and contents insurance'—this most closely matches 'LifeFirst Standard Coverage' (rating 8) under property_protection.home_content, since all 'LifeFirst' products are standard-tier and non-premium. For travel coverage, the plan specifies 'Executive international travel insurance'—the only 'LifeFirst' travel plan in the ground truth is 'LifeFirst Standard Plan' under travel_insurance, which has rating 7. The 'additional_services' described (liability protection, concierge, 24/7 assistance, etc.) align best with 'Premium Insurance Membership' (rating 10) under general_services. Thus, the mapping of selected items to ratings is straightforward once the naming conventions are matched.
